codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the blocking issue from round 4. The non-functional regex `(?<!.)!=(?!=)` has been replaced with `(?<![=])!=(?!=)` in tech-debt-auditor.droid line 62. The new regex uses a character-class negative lookbehind that correctly excludes `!==` while finding real loose `!=` usages. Verified: the command now returns 79 matches across the JS codebase (discovery-wizard-verify.js, user-content-store.js, resume-bundle.js, etc.), all representing genuine loose inequality patterns like `!= null`. The diff is minimal (1 file, 1 line changed) and focused solely on the reported issue.

summary: PASS — the fix adequately addresses the original blocking failure. The non-functional `(?<!.)!=(?!=)` regex has been replaced with `(?<![=])!=(?!=)` which correctly finds loose `!=` operators in the codebase (79 matches verified) without false positives on `!==`. Commit e478af2 is verified to exist and contains exactly the expected single-line change. No remaining issues.
